subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **Modular track tiles** (straights, curves, hills)
- **2 cyclist miniatures per player** (Rouleur and Sprinteur)
- **15 Rouleur cards** per player (values: 3, 3, 3, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 5, 6, 6, 6, 7, 7, 7)
- **15 Sprinteur cards** per player (values: 2, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 5, 9, 9, 9)
- **Exhaustion cards** (value 2)
- **Energy cards** per player deck

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Build track** — Connect track tiles to form the race course.
2. **Mark finish line** on the last tile.
3. **Place cyclists** on the starting grid (positions determined by player order).
4. **Each player** shuffles their Rouleur deck and Sprinteur deck separately.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **2 cyclists** per player: Rouleur (steady) and Sprinteur (explosive).
- **2 separate decks** of 15 cards each.
- **No hand** — Draw 4 each turn.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Flamme Rouge is a cycling race game. Manage two riders (Rouleur and Sprinteur) with separate decks. Play cards to move, draft behind other riders, and avoid exhaustion. First rider across the finish wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
All players act simultaneously:
1. **Draw 4** for your Sprinteur — Pick 1, shuffle rest back.
2. **Draw 4** for your Rouleur — Pick 1, shuffle rest back.
3. **Reveal** — All players reveal chosen cards.
4. **Move** — Move cyclists forward by their card values (front cyclist moves first).
5. **Slipstream** — After movement, check for drafting.
6. **Exhaustion** — Cyclists in front without anyone directly behind gain 1 exhaustion card.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Movement
- Rouleur cards: consistent values (3-7).
- Sprinteur cards: extreme values (2-2-2 or 9-9-9).
- Move forward exactly the number of spaces on the played card.
- Cannot move through occupied spaces; stack behind if blocked.

#### Slipstream (Drafting)
- After all movement, any cyclist with exactly 1 empty space between them and the next cyclist ahead moves forward 1 space for free.
- Check repeatedly until no more slipstream moves are possible.

#### Exhaustion
- Any cyclist who is at the front of a group (no one directly ahead in the same lane or adjacent lane) receives 1 exhaustion card into their deck.
- Exhaustion cards have value 2 (very weak).

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Two lanes** — Track is 2 lanes wide. Cyclists can be side by side.
- **Narrow sections** — Some track sections narrow to 1 lane.
- **Hills** — Uphill: max card value of 5. Downhill: minimum value of 5.
- **Exhaustion dilutes** — More exhaustion cards weaken your deck over time.
- **No deck reshuffling** — When your deck runs out, you only draw from remaining cards.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Winning
- First cyclist (of any player) to cross the finish line wins for their team.
- If multiple cyclists cross on the same turn, the one furthest past the line wins.

#### No points — pure race.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Draft behind others** — Slipstream gives free movement and avoids exhaustion.
- **Save Sprinteur 9s** — Don't waste your sprint cards early.
- **Avoid leading** — Being in front causes exhaustion.
- **Rouleur consistency** — Use the Rouleur for steady progress.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Peloton riding** — Stay in the pack to draft and avoid exhaustion.
- **Sprint timing** — Save high Sprinteur cards for the final stretch.
- **Deck management** — Avoid exhaustion cards to keep your deck efficient.
- **Team coordination** — Use one cyclist to block while the other positions for a breakaway.
